similar_questions.title: Check if Every Row and Column Contains All Numbers

discussion_posts.name: betrayy
discussion_posts.content: Was it just me who couldn\'t figure out the "formula" for knowing which sub-box you\'re in at position (r,c)?

discussion_posts.name: jjjjgggg
discussion_posts.content: This problem is easy at best. The board dimension is fixed 9x9 so every solution is O(1) in theory. Using 27 hash sets would be a no brainer for any one.

discussion_posts.name: astrobot95
discussion_posts.content:
Lot of comments saying this should be easy but I think medium is a fair rating for this problem. It isn't complex (it is basically a brute force problem). But it tests you in other areas of programming like iteration over 2D arrays, iteration over 3x3 grids in the 9x9 array, character to ASCII value conversions etc,.

These things are not particularly trivial.

discussion_posts.content: I find it the hardest to figure out what the box number should be with the row and column number. Is there a better way of thinking about this?
